confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_e12e7240-3685-4841-aa58-740e5d863ad2 read back as promoted

evidence: CPY task create first failed with Unable to acquire registry lock after 10 attempts: ENOENT because .ai/do-not-open/tasks/.registry.lock parent path did not exist.
narada task preflight then failed with ENOENT scandir .ai/do-not-open/tasks until the directory was created manually.
After manual directory creation, task preflight succeeded and task create produced CPY tasks 1-3.
narada inbox pending env_d7dfa... --to task:1 succeeded, but subsequent workboard rendered target as task:task:1.
proposal: bootstrap-windows/client-Site initialization should create or reconcile the canonical task spec directory required by task-governance, or task create/preflight should create it through a sanctioned repair path.
inbox pending should normalize --to task:<n> without later rendering task:task:<n>; target kind and target ref should not both include the kind prefix.
payload.recommendation: Create a Narada proper machinery task covering client Site task-substrate preflight/repair and inbox pending target normalization.
payload.summary: While processing CPY Site builder observations, task creation failed until the architect manually created .ai/do-not-open/tasks. The Site already had .ai/task-lifecycle.db and top-level tasks/chapters directories, so task preflight and task create were not self-repairing enough for a newly bootstrapped client Site. After routing inbox observations with narada inbox pending --to task:1 and --to task:2, workboard displayed targets as task:task:1 and task:task:2, suggesting target kind/ref normalization is duplicated.
payload.title: Client Site task bootstrap and inbox pending target rendering need repair
